title: Week 11 - Sorting Algorithms II and Applications
description: Practice insertion sort, merging sorted arrays, deduplication after sorting, top-three extraction, and tied ranking.

questions.title: Insert a New Score into the Correct Position Like Organizing Playing Cards

description.background: The scores array has already been sorted from small to large. The teacher receives a new score newScore and needs to put it into the correct position while keeping the array sorted.
description.task: Generate the sorted array after inserting the new score.
description.input: One sorted array scores, one number n, and one number newScore.
description.output: Return the new array after insertion.
description.hint: Find where the new score should go, then put the other scores into the new array in order.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n, newScore)
    local result = {}
    local inserted = false
    local r = 1

    for i = 1, n do
        if inserted == false and newScore <= scores[i] then
            result[r] = newScore
            r = r + 1
            inserted = true
        end
        result[r] = scores[i]
        r = r + 1
    end

    if inserted == false then
        result[r] = newScore
    end

    return result
end

questions.title: Find the Middle Score in a Sorted Score Sheet

description.background: The scores array has already been sorted from small to large, and the number of students n is odd. The teacher wants to find the score exactly in the middle.
description.task: Return the middle score in the sorted score sheet.
description.input: One sorted array scores and one odd number n.
description.output: Return the score at the middle position.
description.hint: The middle position depends on the array length.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local mid = (n + 1) / 2
    return scores[mid]
end

questions.title: Count Adjacent Equal Scores After Sorting

description.background: The scores array has already been sorted from small to large. Equal scores will be next to each other, and the teacher wants to know how many times adjacent scores are equal.
description.task: Count how many adjacent positions have the same score.
description.input: One sorted array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the number of adjacent-equal occurrences.
description.hint: You only need to compare the current position with the next position.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        if scores[i] == scores[i + 1] then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Use Insertion Sort to Organize Game Scores

description.background: Player scores are stored in the array scores. The teacher wants to simulate the process of organizing playing cards with insertion sort and sort the scores from small to large.
description.task: Use insertion sort to organize scores.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the scores array sorted from small to large.
description.hint: Each time, insert the current data into the already sorted part before it.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    for i = 2, n do
        local key = scores[i]
        local j = i - 1

        while j >= 1 and scores[j] > key do
            scores[j + 1] = scores[j]
            j = j - 1
        end

        scores[j + 1] = key
    end

    return scores
end

questions.title: Merge Two Already Sorted Score Sheets

description.background: The score sheets classA and classB for two classes are both already sorted from small to large. The teacher wants to merge them into one new sorted score sheet.
description.task: Merge the two sorted arrays while keeping small-to-large order.
description.input: Two sorted arrays classA and classB, and two numbers n and m.
description.hint: You can record the current comparison position in each array separately.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(classA, n, classB, m)
    local result = {}
    local i = 1
    local j = 1
    local r = 1

    while i <= n and j <= m do
        if classA[i] <= classB[j] then
            result[r] = classA[i]
            i = i + 1
        else
            result[r] = classB[j]
            j = j + 1
        end
        r = r + 1
    end

    while i <= n do
        result[r] = classA[i]
        i = i + 1
        r = r + 1
    end

    while j <= m do
        result[r] = classB[j]
        j = j + 1
        r = r + 1
    end

    return result
end

questions.title: Sort Scores and Count How Many Different Scores There Are

description.background: The score array scores is originally unordered. The teacher needs to sort it first, then count how many different score values it contains.
description.task: Sort scores from small to large first, then count the number of distinct scores.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the number of distinct scores.
description.hint: After sorting, equal scores will be next to each other.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    for i = 2, n do
        local key = scores[i]
        local j = i - 1

        while j >= 1 and scores[j] > key do
            scores[j + 1] = scores[j]
            j = j - 1
        end

        scores[j + 1] = key
    end

    if n == 0 then
        return 0
    end

    local count = 1
    for i = 2, n do
        if scores[i] ~= scores[i - 1] then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Handle Tied Ranks in a Score Ranking

description.background: A score ranking needs to be sorted from high to low. If two students have the same score, they receive the same rank; later students skip the positions already occupied. For example, scores 100, 100, 90 have ranks 1, 1, 3.
description.task: Sort scores from high to low first, then generate the tied-rank array for each position.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the sorted scores array and the ranks array in order.
description.hint: Sort first, then decide whether the rank changes based on whether adjacent scores are equal.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        for j = 1, n - i do
            if scores[j] < scores[j + 1] then
                local temp = scores[j]
                scores[j] = scores[j + 1]
                scores[j + 1] = temp
            end
        end
    end

    local ranks = {}
    local currentRank = 1

    for i = 1, n do
        if i == 1 then
            currentRank = 1
        elseif scores[i] < scores[i - 1] then
            currentRank = i
        end
        ranks[i] = currentRank
    end

    return scores, ranks
end

questions.title: Merge Two Sorted Book Number Lists, Remove Duplicates, and Keep Order

description.background: Two libraries provide book number lists that are already sorted from small to large. The two lists may contain duplicate book numbers, and the administrator wants to merge them into one new list without duplicates.
description.task: Merge the two sorted arrays, remove duplicate book numbers, and keep the order from small to large.
description.input: Two sorted arrays booksA and booksB, and two numbers n and m.
description.output: Return the deduplicated number array and the remaining number count in order.
description.hint: You can merge in order while recording only numbers that differ from the previous recorded number.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(booksA, n, booksB, m)
    local result = {}
    local i = 1
    local j = 1
    local r = 0
    local last = 0
    local hasLast = false

    while i <= n or j <= m do
        local value = 0

        if j > m or (i <= n and booksA[i] <= booksB[j]) then
            value = booksA[i]
            i = i + 1
        else
            value = booksB[j]
            j = j + 1
        end

        if hasLast == false or value ~= last then
            r = r + 1
            result[r] = value
            last = value
            hasLast = true
        end
    end

    return result, r
end

questions.title: Find the Top Three After Sorting and Check Whether a New Score Enters the Top Three

description.background: A game leaderboard needs to sort scores from high to low first, then take the top three. Now there is a new score newScore, and the system also needs to decide whether it can enter the top three.
description.task: Sort scores, return the top three scores, and decide whether newScore can enter the top three.
description.input: One array scores, one number n, and one number newScore. n is guaranteed to be at least 3.
description.output: Return first place, second place, third place, and whether newScore can enter the top three in order.
description.hint: Find the current top three first, then compare the new score with third place.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n, newScore)
    for i = 2, n do
        local key = scores[i]
        local j = i - 1

        while j >= 1 and scores[j] < key do
            scores[j + 1] = scores[j]
            j = j - 1
        end

        scores[j + 1] = key
    end

    local canEnter = false
    if newScore >= scores[3] then
        canEnter = true
    end

    return scores[1], scores[2], scores[3], canEnter
end
